events.text: Patient is a 45-year-old female of Black/African American race with an ECOG performance status of 1
At age 45, patient presented with a large palpable left inguinal mass, abdominal distension, and drenching night sweats for three weeks
At age 45, excisional biopsy of the left inguinal lymph node revealed diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, ICD-O site C77.9, histology code 9680/3

At age 45, pathology report showed large centroblastic and immunoblastic cells with high mitotic activity, Ki-67 of 90 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, BCL6, MUM1, negative for CD10, consistent with activated B-cell (ABC/non-GCB) subtype by Hans algorithm
At age 45, flow cytometry showed monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, BCL6, and MUM1, negative for CD10, CD5, consistent with non-GCB phenotype

At age 45, FISH analysis revealed MYC rearrangement positive, BCL2 rearrangement positive, BCL6 rearrangement negative; classified as double-hit lymphoma, reclassified as high-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements
At age 45, laboratory workup showed LDH markedly elevated at 612 U/L, CBC with WBC 11.4, hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL, platelets 165, CMP revealed elevated uric acid at 9.1 mg/dL

At age 45, staging PET/CT revealed bulky FDG-avid bilateral inguinal, retroperitoneal, and mesenteric lymphadenopathy with largest retroperitoneal mass measuring 9.8 cm, SUVmax 28.6, diffuse FDG uptake in bone marrow suggesting involvement
At age 45, bone marrow biopsy showed 30 percent involvement by large B-cell lymphoma, confirmed by immunohistochemistry positive for CD20 and BCL2
At age 45, patient was staged as Ann Arbor Stage IV with B symptoms due to bone marrow involvement, IPI score 4 (high risk) based on elevated LDH, Stage IV, bone marrow involvement, and ECOG 1
At age 45, patient initiated cycle 1 of R-CHOP with tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis including rasburicase, allopurinol, and aggressive hydration given high tumor burden
At age 45, during cycle 1 of R-CHOP, patient developed grade 3 neutropenic fever requiring hospitalization and IV broad-spectrum antibiotics with piperacillin-tazobactam for 5 days
At age 45, patient received cycle 2 of R-CHOP with dose reduction of cyclophosphamide by 25 percent due to prior neutropenic fever, with pegfilgrastim support
At age 45, patient received cycle 3 of R-CHOP with continued dose-reduced cyclophosphamide

At age 45, interim PET/CT after 3 cycles showed persistent FDG-avid retroperitoneal mass now measuring 7.1 cm with SUVmax 15.2, Deauville score 5, indicating refractory disease with suboptimal response
At age 45, given refractory disease on interim PET/CT with double-hit biology, decision was made to complete planned 6 cycles of R-CHOP followed by early salvage therapy
At age 45, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of R-CHOP with increasing neuropathy symptoms
At age 45, after cycle 5, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with numbness and pain in hands and feet, vincristine reduced by 50 percent for cycle 6
At age 45, end-of-treatment PET/CT showed residual FDG-avid retroperitoneal mass measuring 4.3 cm, SUVmax 9.8, Deauville score 5, confirming primary refractory DLBCL
At age 45, patient initiated salvage therapy with R-ICE: rituximab 375 mg/m2, ifosfamide 5000 mg/m2, carboplatin AUC 5, etoposide 100 mg/m2 days 1-3
At age 45, after cycle 1 of R-ICE, patient developed grade 4 neutropenia and grade 3 thrombocytopenia requiring platelet transfusion and growth factor support
At age 45, patient completed 3 cycles of R-ICE salvage therapy
At age 45, PET/CT after R-ICE salvage showed partial response with residual FDG-avid retroperitoneal node measuring 2.1 cm, Deauville score 4
At age 46, patient underwent successful autologous stem cell collection with adequate CD34+ cell yield of 5.2 million cells/kg
At age 46, patient received BEAM conditioning (carmustine, etoposide, cytarabine, melphalan) followed by autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplant
At age 46, post-transplant course complicated by grade 3 mucositis and febrile neutropenia requiring IV antibiotics for 10 days
At age 46, patient engrafted on day 12 post-transplant with ANC recovery above 500, discharged on day 18
At age 46, PET/CT at day 100 post-transplant showed complete metabolic response, Deauville score 2, consistent with complete remission

scenario_blurb: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center and activated B-cell subtypes. Ann Arbor stage II-IV. Treated with R-CHOP. Include IPI scoring, PET-CT response assessment, some with relapse requiring R-ICE salvage and autologous stem cell transplant.
